message:
perf(btree): skip CellRef::parse for predecessor_idx rowid-only read

bd-9e3xf cache-miss audit showed MISS_INVALIDATED at 99% on the DELETE
workload — the cache cannot help predecessor/separator lookups because
every delete bumps the page image_token and invalidates whatever was
cached. For this class of caller, the right move is to bypass the cache
entirely and use a specialised parse helper that only computes what the
caller actually reads.

`BtCursor::predecessor_idx` is on the DELETE rebalance path and only
reads `cell.rowid` from the returned `CellRef`. The generic
`CellRef::parse` for a LeafTable cell decoded the payload_size varint,
the rowid varint, ran `local_payload_size` math, and checked overflow
bounds — all thrown away here. The cell-slot cache insert that follows
a miss is also wasted since the same (page, image_token) will never be
queried again before the next delete invalidates it.

Adds `CellRef::parse_leaf_table_rowid(page, cell_offset) -> Result<i64>`
in cell.rs that reads exactly two varints (payload_size length for
positioning, then the rowid itself) with one bounds check per varint.

Wires the predecessor_idx call site to read the cell pointer inline and
call the rowid-only helper — same "caller only needs one field"
pattern shipped in:

- 4b061dcc: child_page_at → read_interior_child_inline (5.41x)
- 385591f8: replace_separator left_child → read_interior_child_inline
            (5.50x)

Measured delta (release microbench, 128 cells, 5M iters):
Before: 6.474 ns / call (CellRef::parse + cell.rowid.ok_or(..))
After:  3.216 ns / call (parse_leaf_table_rowid direct)
Delta:  2.01x speedup on the parse step alone; the cache round-trip
        (~4–8 ns) on the current call site is also eliminated because
        the rowid helper doesn't touch CellSlotCache.
Bead:   bd-9e3xf (classification audit, this is the concrete
                  MISS_INVALIDATED-lane fix derived from its data)
Parity: fsqlite-btree lib compiles clean; cell.rs change is additive
        (new pub helper, no API break); cursor.rs change swaps one
        caller from cached full-parse to direct rowid extract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
